data.title: Orvana Minerals announces director appointments
data.description:
• Orvana Minerals (TSX:ORV:CA) announced that Michael Davies, Alfredo Garcia Gonzalez, Robert Metcalfe and Michael Mutchler were elected as directors of the company. 
  • Robert Metcalfe was re-appointed as chairman of the board. 
  • The company will also re-appoint PricewaterhouseCoopers as the company's independent auditor for the ensuing year. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Corvex takes new bitcoin bet with IBIT, exits Hexcel, among Q4 top moves
data.description:
Keith Meister's Corvex Management took new stakes Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), with 201K shares, and iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT), with 1.18M shares, during Q4 2024, the hedge fund disclosed in a 13F filing Friday.

Meanwhile, it closed its position in Hexcel Corp. (HXL) during the three-month period.

The firm also boosted its holdings in CSX Corp. (CSX), to 1.75M shares from 1.04M shares, IAC Inc. (IAC), to 2.41M shares from 1.49M shares, and Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp.(AQN), to 3.35M shares from 3.04M shares.

And it lowered its stake in Illumina (ILMN), to 1.7M shares from 2.28M shares.

data.title: Eminence Capital adds GE Healthcare, exits Smartsheet, among Q4 trades
data.description:
Eminence Capital added GE Healthcare (NASDAQ:GEHC), Advance Auto (NYSE:AAP), and Freshworks (NASDAQ:FRSH) to its portfolio in Q4 2024, while exiting Smartsheet (SMAR), CBRE (NYSE:CBRE), and Monday.com (NASDAQ:MNDY), it disclosed in its 13F filing on Friday.

The firm acquired 1.31M shares of GE Healthcare (NASDAQ:GEHC) with a value of $102.6M at Dec. 31, 204; 1.84M shares of Advance Auto (NYSE:AAP) with a value of $87.2M; and ~5.32M shares of Freshworks (NASDAQ:FRSH) with a value of $86.0M.

The company increased its stakes in Elevance (ELV) to 555.5M shares (value $204.9M) from 231K; Atmus Filtration (ATMU) to 4.59M shares (value $179.9M) from 2.56M shares; and Ferguson Enterprises (FERG) to ~778K shares (value $135.0M) from ~296K.

Among stakes it pared: Charles River (CRL) to 627K shares (value $115.8M) from 1.00M; LPL Finanicial (LPLA) to ~559K shares (value $182.4M) from ~1.08M; and Corteva (CTVA) to 2.36M shares (value $134.7M) from 3.16M, according to the filing.

data.title: Druckenmiller's Duquesne adds airlines, exits Microsoft, Broadcom, others
data.description:
Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office closed out holdings in Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Broadcom (AVGO), Mid-America (MAA), and Camden Property (CPT), according to its latest 13F filing.

New positions include 1.04 million shares in United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), 818K in Delta Air (DAL), 2.5M in SLM Corp (SLM), and new stakes in Amazon (AMZN) and Skechers (SKX).

The firm increased positions in Teva (TEVA), US Steel (X) and Insmed (INSM), according to the Q4 filing.

The billionaire's family office reduced stakes in Coupang (CPNG) by 2.8M, Flutter (FLUT), Seagate (STX), and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) by 618K shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities appoints interim CFO
data.description:
• Algonquin Power & Utilities (TSX:AQN:CA) announced that vice president of investor relations Brian Chin will assume the role of interim chief financial officer, effective March 7, 2025.
  • The announcement follows an earlier announcement of the pending departure of current CFO, Darren Myers.
  • Algonquin has engaged a search firm to commence a search to identify a permanent CFO.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Wheat prices pop as cold weather threatens winter crops
data.description:
U.S. wheat futures surged Friday on the Chicago Board of Trade, with a lack of snow cover seen leaving U.S. winter wheat vulnerable to below-freezing temperatures.

Highs in the Plains state are expected to be well below freezing over the weekend, with subzero highs expected in North Dakota, and traders are concerned about the bitter cold killing winter wheat crops because of a lack of snow cover; however, a storm system also is forecast to move across the U.S., which could provide new snow cover for affected crops.

CBOT wheat for March delivery (W_1:COM) finished +4.1% to $6.01 1/2 per bushel, while March soybeans (S_1:COM) settled +0.7% to $10.36 3/4 per bushel and March corn (C_1:COM) closed +0.6% to $4.96 1/2 per bushel.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (DBA), (MOO)

President Trump's lack of immediate reciprocal tariffs was taken as a positive in the U.S. grain market, since the action appears to have been aimed mostly at bringing countries to the negotiating table rather than immediate pain.

Grain futures continue to benefit from less than ideal weather conditions in Argentina, where the Rosario Grain Exchange lowered its outlook for corn production by 2M metric tons to 46M and for soybean output by 2.5M tons to 47.5M tons.

Wheat futures have surged nearly $0.60 per bushel since mid-January, with cold weather expected to cause a supply squeeze for Russian crops following a series of large crops and exports in recent years; some observers believe Russia’s current crop may be the smallest in more than a decade.

"Wheat is... finding support from reductions to the Russian crop and building concerns over the Indian crop," Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting said. "India is already seeing its wheat supplies drop and now another short crop will likely lead to imports."

data.title: MGO Global stockholders approve business combination with Heidmar; shares tumble
data.description:
• MGO Global (NASDAQ:MGOL) announced that MGO stockholders approved the previously announced proposed business combination with Heidmar.
  • The closing of the business combination is expected to occur next week.
  • The combined company will operate under the name Heidmar Maritime Holdings Corporation, and its ordinary shares are expected to trade on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “HMR”.
  • MGOL shares tumble 22% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Ameren signals 9.2% rate base growth CAGR and $63B investment pipeline through 2029
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ameren Corporation (NYSE:AEE) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Martin Lyons highlighted strong operational and financial results for 2024, including adjusted earnings of $4.63 per share, exceeding the midpoint of their guidance range. He emphasized a $4.3 billion investment in energy infrastructure, timely regulatory approvals, and prudent cost management.
  • Ameren's updated five-year investment plan includes a $26.3 billion capital expenditure budget, a 20% increase over the previous plan, primarily driven by accelerated investments in generation and infrastructure.
  • CFO Michael Moehn reported that weather-normalized retail sales in Missouri increased 2% in 2024, with industrial sales up 3%, reflecting strong regional economic growth. He stated, "We made significant strides in cost management, with Ameren Missouri’s O&M expenses down $12 million year-over-year, excluding one-time charges."

Outlook

  • Ameren provided 2025 adjusted earnings guidance in the range of $4.85 to $5.05 per share, with the midpoint representing 7% growth over 2024 results. Management projects a 6% to 8% compound annual earnings growth rate from 2025 through 2029.
  • Lyons stated that weather-normalized retail sales are now expected to grow at a 5.5% compound annual growth rate from 2025 through 2029, significantly above prior expectations of flat to up 0.5%.
  • The company anticipates $63 billion in investment opportunities over the next decade, driven by robust rate base growth of 9.2% annually.

Financial Results

  • Ameren reported 2024 adjusted earnings of $4.63 per share, up from $4.38 per share in 2023. The increase was attributed to strategic infrastructure investments and strong weather-normalized retail sales growth in Missouri.
  • Year-to-date weather-normalized sales grew across all customer classes in Missouri, with residential, commercial, and industrial sales rising 2%, 1.5%, and 3%, respectively.
  • The company’s $4.3 billion infrastructure investment in 2024 included updates to 250 miles of power lines under Ameren Missouri’s Smart Energy Plan, contributing to improved reliability and grid resilience.

Q&A

  • Shahriar Pourreza, Guggenheim Securities: Questioned whether Ameren's sales growth and rate base expansion could lead to exceeding the upper end of the 6% to 8% EPS growth range. Martin Lyons responded, "We expect to deliver near the upper end of the range in the mid to latter part of the plan."
  • Carly Davenport, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the updated integrated resource plan and potential nuclear investments. Lyons explained that Ameren is evaluating new nuclear options for 2040, including small modular reactors and traditional technologies, but no material financial commitments are expected in the near term.
  • Durgesh Chopra, Evercore: Inquired about balance sheet strength given the increased capital plan. Moehn noted, "We feel very good about our balance sheet. We are at or above the 17% FFO-to-debt threshold required by Moody’s for Baa1 ratings."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about the robust sales growth and capital investment pipeline but raised questions about execution risks and balance sheet management. Their tone was positive but cautiously optimistic.
  • Management maintained a confident and assertive tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong operational results and actionable strategies for growth. During Q&A, the tone remained consistent, with detailed responses highlighting preparedness for future challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more confident, reflecting the significant upward revision of sales growth expectations and expanded capital investment plans.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance expectations for 2025 remained consistent with the prior quarter, but the updated forecast for a 5.5% retail sales CAGR from 2025 through 2029 marked a major upward revision.
  • The five-year capital expenditure plan increased by 20%, driven by accelerated generation and infrastructure investments, compared to the previously disclosed plan.
  • Management’s tone during Q&A was more assertive, focusing on the company’s ability to execute larger-scale projects and manage cost efficiencies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the need for regulatory approvals for tariffs and generation investments related to new industrial demand and data center expansions.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential execution risks tied to the ambitious $63 billion investment pipeline and the ability to maintain a strong balance sheet amid rising debt issuances.

Final Takeaway

Ameren Corporation delivered strong financial results for 2024 and raised its long-term growth outlook, driven by robust sales growth and an expanded $63 billion investment pipeline. With a revised five-year capital plan of $26.3 billion and expectations of a 9.2% rate base growth CAGR, the company is positioned to capitalize on significant industrial demand and economic growth in its service territories. Management’s confidence in achieving the upper end of its 6% to 8% EPS growth range underscores the execution of its strategic priorities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oncolytics Biotech receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Oncolytics Biotech (NASDAQ:ONCY) received a delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq indicating that the company is not currently in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • ONCY has a period of 180 calendar days from the date of the notice, or until August 12, 2025, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Elliott Investment Management exits Cardinal Health, cuts NRG Energy, Southwest Air
data.description:
Famed activist investor Elliott Investment Management exited its position in Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH), dumping 2M shares in Q4, according to its 13F filing.

The hedge fund increased its stake in CorMedix (CRMD) by 1.3M shares and added to its stake in HDFC Bank (HDB), according to the filing on Friday.

Elliott decreased its position in NRG Energy (NRG) shedding 4.9M shares and its position in Southwest Air (LUV), trimming 1.45M shares, according to the 13F filing.
